subject: Human Resources
title: The Rise of Employee Benefits
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Rise of Employee Benefits usable.

hook.text: During WWII, the U.S. froze wages. Companies couldn't raise pay—so they offered health insurance instead.

buildup.text: Employer-sponsored benefits exploded: health insurance, pensions, paid vacation, and life insurance became standard.

discovery.text: Benefits became a key competitive tool—companies used them to attract and retain talent when pay was capped.

twist.text: This wartime workaround is why Americans get health insurance from employers—a system unique in the world.

climax.text: Benefits administration became one of HR's most complex and costly responsibilities—and remains so today.

punchline.text: A wartime wage freeze created employer-based healthcare.

quiz.question: Why did employer-sponsored health insurance become common in the U.S.?
options: WWII wage freezes pushed companies to offer benefits instead
